summary: Package installs a.pth file that runs os.system on every Python interpreter start
details: The package ships `suspicious-demo.pth` which Python automatically processes on every interpreter startup once installed into site-packages. The.pth file contains `import os; os.system("calc.exe"); print("Hacked by LamentXU!")` and also imports `spip_pth_demo_marker`, whose module body executes `os.system("calc.exe")` again. This means every `python` invocation on the installer's machine — including unrelated scripts, build steps, and CI jobs — triggers an arbitrary shell command. While the demonstrated payload (calc.exe) is benign, the mechanism is arbitrary OS command execution persisted via a.pth side-effect, which is one of the most aggressive forms of installer-side code execution. The README falsely claims the package 'does not launch processes or run OS commands' and contains a self-incriminating banner string ('Hacked by LamentXU!'), confirming intent to deceive installers. Any package that establishes persistent shell execution on every Python start, while documenting itself as inert, is unsafe to allow regardless of the specific command shipped today.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
